arts.ttl: Everton v Peterborough, Fulham v Watford, and more: FA Cup third round – live
arts.sum: Sean Dyche sacked as Everton manager by new owners Live scoreboard | Read Football Daily | And email Taha Speaking of the Young v Young battle, Luke McLaughlin has more on footballing father and sons. This is a really lovely email, sent in by Nicholas Byrne. Toffee here. Just want to put on record my thanks to Sean Dyche and his team. When he was appointed, I wrote that we’d finally employed a grown up, and I say that as someone who really wanted Lampard to succeed, and really really wanted Rafa to succeed. I’m firmly in the mind that nobody but Dyche could have got us through our points deductions, which were definitely the club’s fault but most definitely not his, and he has handled himself with dignity throughout his tenure. It won’t be any consolation to him tonight, but this Everton fan will always be grateful to him. As for who is next, the new owners have already taken one major decision, we need to trust them to get the next one right, too. Onwards and sideways, Blues. Continue reading...

arts.sum: Watford goalkeeper Daniel Bachmann picked up an injury during the game against Sheffield United at the weekend. The 30-year-old Austrian is expected to be out of action for some months and this has put the goalkeeper situation at Vicarage Road in uncertainty. arts.ttl: Fulham vs Watford: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Fulham are in FA Cup third round action on Thursday as they take on Watford at Craven Cottage. Watford have an excellent record against their upcoming opponents, but they head into Thursday's cup tie in pretty wretched form, having initially made a strong start to the Championship season.
